worn spindle bearing (DE side), grease breakdown
low gearbox oil due to slow leak at output seal
mechanical seal worn out past service life
worn discharge valve plates leaking back into cylinder
suction strainer partially blocked causing cavitation

Recurring gearbox overheating from low lubricating oil — most likely a slow leak at the gearbox output seal, matching the previous failure pattern documented in WO-1011. The temperature has climbed steadily (+18.5°C drift) from ~49°C to 80.69°C while vibration, pressure, and RPM all remain stable, indicating a pure thermal/lubrication problem rather than a mechanical binding or misalignment issue.
